title: Czech Republic World Cup 2026 squad: Narodak name 26-man squad to head to North America | Squawka | EN
original_text:
Czech Republic World Cup 2026 squad: Narodak name 26-man squad to head to North America | Squawka
Andy Francis
4 weeks ago
Czech Republic have named their 26-man squad ahead of the 2026 World Cup as they look to emerge from Group A.
Miroslav Koubek is the man who will lead Czech Republic into the World Cup and, after being appointed in December 2025, was able to navigate the Uefa playoffs to secure his nations place in North America.
The Czech Republic squad isn’t the most well-known but there are a couple of players that will catch the eye including Ladislav Krejčí of Wolves and West Ham’s Tomáš Souček.

The Czech Republic arrive at the 2026 FIFA World Cup with a squad that perfectly balances seasoned campaigners and exciting young talent, a testament to their robust footballing development. While they may not boast a global superstar like some nations, their strength lies in a cohesive unit, underpinned by a core of experienced players.
Leading the charge are stalwarts such as Vladimír Coufal, Tomáš Souček, Vladimír Darida, and Patrik Schick, each having amassed over 50 international caps. Their leadership and tactical nous will be invaluable in navigating the pressures of the tournament. Furthermore, players like Tomáš Holeš and Adam Hložek are on the cusp of reaching the half-century mark, ready to step up and contribute significantly.
Complementing this experience is a vibrant contingent of six promising youngsters, all aged 23 or under, including Lukáš Horníček, Štěpán Chaloupek, and the highly-rated Hložek. These emerging talents bring youthful exuberance, pace, and a hunger to make their mark on the world stage, ensuring the Czech Republic possess both the wisdom and the energy to surprise their opponents.
Read more: Czech Republic World Cup 2026 odds
Three players to look out for this summer
Patrik Schick (Bayer Leverkusen)
As the Czech Republic prepare for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, all eyes will undoubtedly be on their talismanic striker, Patrik Schick. At 30 years old, the Bayer Leverkusen forward is at the peak of his powers, having enjoyed a sensational 2025/26 season that saw him lead his club to domestic glory and contribute an impressive 16 goals in 28 Bundesliga matches. Schick’s clinical finishing and tireless work rate make him the undisputed spearhead of the Czech attack, a role he has embraced with 25 goals in 52 appearances for his nation.
Schick’s ability to find the back of the net from various positions, coupled with his imposing physical presence and intelligent movement, makes him a constant threat to opposition defences. While his individual brilliance is undeniable, he also possesses the tactical awareness to link up effectively with his teammates, creating opportunities and drawing defenders. For the Czech Republic, Schick is not merely a goalscorer; he is the focal point around whom much of their attacking play revolves, and his form will be paramount to their aspirations on the global stage. When Schick is firing, the Czechs are a force to be reckoned with.
Adam Hložek (Hoffenheim)
Adam Hložek, the prodigious Czech forward, arrives at the 2026 FIFA World Cup as one of his nation’s most exciting prospects. Despite a season hampered by injury, the 23-year-old has made a remarkable recovery. His versatility, capable of playing across the front line or as an attacking midfielder, makes him a vital asset for his country, offering both creativity and a keen eye for goal.
Hložek’s journey has been marked by early promise and a rapid ascent through the ranks. With 42 international appearances and five goals already to his name, he brings a blend of youthful exuberance and valuable experience to the squad. While he may not yet possess the sheer volume of caps as some of his more seasoned teammates, his impact on the pitch is undeniable. His ability to link play, drive forward with the ball, and contribute to crucial attacking movements will be pivotal for the Czech Republic’s aspirations in the tournament. When Hložek is on form, his dynamic presence can unlock even the most resolute defences.
Pavel Šulc (Lyon)
Pavel Šulc arrives at the 2026 FIFA World Cup as a dynamic attacking midfielder, a crucial component in the Czech Republic’s quest for glory. Having recently completed a significant move to Ligue 1 giants Olympique Lyon in August 2025, Šulc has continued to impress, showcasing his creative flair and goal-scoring ability. His 2025/26 season saw him net 11 goals and provide 3 assists in Ligue 1, demonstrating his consistent threat in the final third.
At 25 years old, Šulc is a versatile player, equally adept as a central attacking midfielder, a box-to-box presence, or even a striker when called upon. His impressive record of 36 goals and 24 assists in 115 league appearances for Viktoria Plzeň prior to his move to Lyon highlights his consistent output and influence. For the Czech national team, Šulc offers a vital attacking spark and a natural inventiveness in the final third, providing the squad with an additional dimension in their offensive play. His ability to unlock defences and contribute directly to goals will be paramount as the Czech Republic aim to make a deep run in the tournament. When Šulc is on the pitch, his creativity and eye for goal can be the difference-maker.
